title: Persistent Semantic Memory for AI Assistants
subtitle: Semantic memory lets an assistant retrieve relevant past context instead of treating every conversation as isolated.
summary: The personal assistant project uses embeddings and ChromaDB-style retrieval for preferences, files, and past conversations.

artifact.title: Memory retrieval as ranked context
artifact.description: A memory system retrieves the past facts most relevant to the current request.

sections.heading: Memory as Retrieval
body: The assistant should not dump all past history into every prompt. It should retrieve a small set of relevant memory records.
That makes memory closer to search than to a giant transcript.

sections.heading: What Gets Stored
body: Useful records include durable preferences, facts the user explicitly wants remembered, summaries of projects, and indexed documents. Temporary chatter should usually expire or remain unpromoted.

pitfalls: Retrieving memories without timestamps or source context.
Letting stale preferences override newer user intent.

quiz.question: Why rank memories before using them?
options: To keep only relevant context in the prompt
quiz.explanation: Ranked retrieval keeps the active context focused.
